questions.prompt: What practice management, tax, audit, and accounting software are you currently using, and what version?
Are your systems cloud-hosted, on-premises, or hybrid? Who controls the data backups?
Do you use a single integrated platform or separate systems for different functions (for example, tax separate from bookkeeping)?
What is your primary database engine (SQL Server, Access, proprietary flat files)?
Can you export your data, and in what formats (CSV, XML, JSON, fixed-width, API)?
How do you uniquely identify a client — is there a client ID, EIN/SSN, or is it name-based?
Do you track multiple entities under a single client/family relationship? If so, how is that hierarchy represented?
Are there merged, duplicate, or archived clients in your system we should know about?
What tax return types do you prepare (1040, 1065, 1120, 1120S, 990, 709, etc.)?
How are tax years and filing jurisdictions tracked — do you handle multi-state returns?
Where do you store prior-year return data, and how many years back is it digitized?
How are extensions, amendments, and superseding returns distinguished in your system?
Are electronic filing records (acknowledgements, e-file history) stored in the practice management system or separately?
What is the structure of your chart of accounts — how many segments, and what do they represent (department, location, fund)?
Are accounts standardized across all clients or customized per engagement?
How is inter-company or consolidation data handled?
Are trial balance and general ledger exports available, and at what level of detail?
How are staff members identified — by name, employee ID, or license number?
How are staff assigned to clients and engagements, and is that relationship tracked historically?
How are workflow statuses defined (for example, In Preparation, In Review, Filed)? Is there a custom status list?
Are due dates and compliance deadlines tracked, and are they tied to return type or manually entered?
What billing rates and time-tracking data exists, and is it tied to specific engagements or clients?
What AR aging buckets do you use, and are they system-defined or custom?
Where are client documents stored — integrated DMS, network share, SharePoint, third-party like Canopy or SmartVault?
Are documents tagged to a specific client, return year, and document type in a consistent taxonomy?
What naming convention is used for files, and is it enforced systematically or ad hoc?
Are there signed engagement letters or authorization forms that need to be migrated and linked to client records?
Are there any known data quality issues — duplicate records, missing EINs, inconsistent naming?
Has your data ever been migrated from a prior system? If so, are there known legacy artifacts or gaps from that migration?
What custom fields, local workarounds, or client-specific attributes does the team rely on that are not covered by standard source-system fields?
Which teams, workflows, reports, or billing/tax processes depend on these custom fields today?
